title: Establishment of protocol for nodule sampling
author: Tampakaki Anastasia P., Efstathiadou Evdoxia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Nodules are specific structure on legume roots, where the reduction of N 2  gas to ammonia takes place. Nitrogen fixation is an enzymatic process, associated with symbiotic nitrogen-fixing bacteria (rhizobia). Nodulation is a complex process, genetically controlled by both partners of the symbiotic relationship as well as by the environment through its influence on the activity of each partner and on the interaction itself. Nodules collection is a very important procedure for isolation of effective rhizobia and it is affected by the growth state of legumes. Nodules are collected either directly from field-grown legumes or indirectly from soil collected from legume rhizosphere using a trap host grown under controlled greenhouse conditions
